response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced engineer (~18 years) with genuine strengths in testing culture, developer tooling, and some high-throughput systems work (low-latency microservices at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic, Kubernetes-deployed platform as a founder). However, he falls well short of the core minimum qualifications: C++ experience is limited to a ~7-month stint at National Instruments and a thesis project versus the required 8 years, his large-scale infrastructure exposure is closer to 2-3 years than 5, and he has no formal technical leadership role leading teams or mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers. His career centers on web/full-stack work in TypeScript, Node.js, and D rather than cloud compute platform engineering, making him a poor fit for this specific role despite solid general seniority.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec) at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong software craftsmanship background: TDD, CI/CD, open-source authorship, and teaching/workshop experience
Broad polyglot experience across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure (Docker, Kubernetes, AWS CDK)
concerns: Far below the required 8 years of C++; primary languages are TypeScript, Node.js, and D
No formal technical leadership role managing project priorities or mentoring engineers across teams
Limited production cloud-architecture and large-scale distributed-systems depth relative to the 5-year requirement
